title: Desktop Path 5 native Ghostty underlay rollout
description: Implement the paseo fork architecture for Maestro Desktop using libghostty + GhosttyKit under a transparent Chromium compositor in apps/desktop. Preserve the existing sidebar, tabs, and git panel; keep maestro panels read-only through CLI JSON; never restart the daemon on port 6767 without explicit user permission. Phase 5 is a hard gate before any agent-pane replacement.

title: Phase 0: verify env and stock paseo desktop dev flow
description: Verify sw_vers, xcode-select, xcodebuild, zig, node, and npm; install workspace deps if needed; confirm stock dev:server, dev:app, and dev:desktop can be launched without restarting the main daemon on port 6767. Acceptance: stock paseo opens, port 6767 is reachable, and no console errors appear.

title: Phase 1: build pinned GhosttyKit xcframework
description: Create packages/ghostty-bridge scaffolding, clone manaflow-ai/ghostty at 6d7bbd23b869ea0a28049e989a55a78932729948, and build GhosttyKit.xcframework via zig build -Demit-xcframework. Acceptance: libghostty.a exists, ghostty.h headers are present, and nm shows _ghostty_init. If the xcframework build fails, stop the rollout and diagnose before proceeding.

title: Phase 2: scaffold ghostty-bridge N-API addon
description: Add the ghostty-bridge workspace package with node-gyp, node-addon-api, TypeScript wrapper, and Objective-C++ sources for runtime, surface, input, and NSView hosting. Acceptance: npm install succeeds, npm run build in packages/ghostty-bridge produces ghostty_bridge.node, and initRuntime() smoke-tests cleanly with no dyld errors.

title: Phase 3: embed Ghostty underlay surface in Electron
description: Make the desktop BrowserWindow transparent, initialize the ghostty runtime on app startup, register ghostty IPC handlers, add renderer-side typed IPC wrappers, and create a GhosttyPanel that manages native surface creation, resize, and teardown. Acceptance: a ghostty tab renders a Metal-backed surface whose theme visibly reflects the user ghostty config.

title: Phase 4: route key, mouse, and focus input to libghostty
description: Wire keyboard, mouse, and focus events from the transparent renderer overlay into libghostty using main-thread dispatch. Acceptance: echo hello, backspace, arrows, focus changes, and mouse selection all work in the embedded surface. IME support remains explicitly deferred.

title: Phase 5: run hard spike gate and stop on failure
description: Execute the spike gate exactly as planned: fresh launch, ghostty tab, user theme/font/background match, kitten icat inline image rendering, ls and claude command viability, resize reflow, and clean Cmd-Q shutdown with no orphan ghostty processes. Any failure blocks all follow-on UI surgery until diagnosed and re-planned.

title: Phase 6: replace agent pane UI with GhosttyPanel
description: Replace the existing agent stream and composer stack with the native GhosttyPanel while preserving paseo session storage semantics. Remove composer, model picker, reasoning, and voice UI from the desktop agent surface. Acceptance: every session tab shows the native terminal, sidebar/tabs/git panel still work, and npm run typecheck exits 0.

title: Phase 7: add per-session agent CLI spawn commands
description: Extend surface creation to accept an optional start command and expose session presets for shell, claude, codex, and maestro worker start <track>. Acceptance: each new terminal session launches the configured command and the per-session default is preserved in the in-memory store for v0.1.

title: Phase 8: add read-only Maestro state panels via CLI JSON
description: Add mission, handoff, tasks, principles, and graph panels that read only from maestro CLI JSON subprocesses and react to debounced .maestro change events. Acceptance: all five panels open, refresh within 2 seconds of relevant state changes, and perform no direct renderer writes to .maestro runtime files.

title: Final integration verification for Path 5 rollout
description: Run the full integration verification matrix after the post-spike implementation lands: stock desktop behavior, native terminal replacement, tab and git panel stability, typecheck, format, and maestro panel refresh behavior.

title: Audit and correct product-risk notes for release/versioning, typecheck, automation scope, and refactor status
closeReason: Completed factual audit of risk notes; verified repo, CI, README, changelog, release page, build, and typecheck; produced corrected copy.

title: Fix audited issues: typecheck failures and versioning/docs inconsistencies
closeReason: Fixed repo typecheck failures, removed command-test module mock leakage, aligned release metadata to 0.35.4, and verified build/tests/render/local install.
